comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the Ichinichi app, with users expressing their thoughts on its design, functionality, and features. Comments range from praise for its concept to inquiries about technical aspects like encryption and user flow.

comments.author: thatgurjot
comments.text: Love it! The name, the design, the concept, the open source codebase, everything! It’s less like a note taking app and more like a d...
comments.analysis: This comment provides enthusiastic support and constructive feedback, highlighting the app's strengths and suggesting a related tool.

comments.author: redgridtactical
comments.text: The read-only past is a really smart design choice. I build local-first apps and it's always tempting to add edit-everything flexib...
comments.analysis: This comment offers a thoughtful analysis of the app's design choices, demonstrating an understanding of local-first principles.

comments.author: kaz-inc
comments.text: I really like the idea, and I've actually built something similar.  Please format the writing in the post sound less gpt-esque; I b...
comments.analysis: The comment expresses support while also providing constructive criticism regarding the writing style, which could enhance clarity.

comments.author: thomasfrank09
comments.text: Very cool! I'm curious as to why you removed ProseMirror after trying it out. I've been building my own writing app for a diff...
comments.analysis: This comment shows curiosity and engagement with the app's development process, though it lacks a direct evaluation of the app itself.

comments.author: jcynix
comments.text: Nice, and I like the idea that the past is fixed, but ... is there a way to define the point of rollover to the next day? My "days"...
comments.analysis: While the comment raises a valid question about user experience, it does not provide substantial feedback on the app's overall value.

comments.author: elxr
comments.text:
How does the E2E work in terms of user flow? I assume a you need a password?

Do you need to enter the password every time you open this?
comments.analysis: This comment seeks clarification on technical aspects but lacks an assessment of the app's features or usability.
